commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Open. Or approved. (Menochius)
Hebrew, "pure in his own eyes. "He sees not his own defects, chap. xxi. 2., and Job xxviii. 23. (Calmet)

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh All the ways of a man are clean in his own eyes; but the LORD weighs the spirits. Besides being bright, certainly light (as it is) is conspicuous. As it does its work everyone observes it. In the same way, the humble person shows us in no small way what is right. For a contrite person will nevertheless excel in great things. But God does not desire to acknowledge the deeds of the proud. Commentary on the Proverbs of Solomon, Fragment.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
